exemplar.title: To what extent has Airbus effectively leveraged marketing strategies to recover its reputation following the delivery delays of the A350?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Airbus effectively leveraged marketing strategies to recover its reputation following the delivery delays of the A350?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

versions.error: Durable Object reset because its code was updated.

strengths: Consistent, explicit integration of the key concept âchangeâ throughout research question, methodology, and analysis sections
Clear connection of change to Airbusâs supply-chain setbacks and reputational recovery
Organization-specific examples (A350 delays, Paris Air Show, Emirates order) anchored in the concept
weaknesses: Introduction does not explicitly link Airbus context to the concept of change
Analysis sometimes relies on repeated tagging of âchangeâ rather than deeper, sustained analytical chains
Financial ratio discussion only lightly connected back to reputational change
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this criterion at band 4 for both understanding/integration and organizational connection. The concept of change is central and well applied, but the integration is not yet fully woven through every analytical chain, falling short of the seamless depth required for band 5.

strengths: Five clearly labelled supporting documents satisfy the quantity requirement
Annual reports and supply-chain visual provide robust quantitative and qualitative evidence
weaknesses: Some blog-style sources lack depth and academic rigor
Appendices not always explicitly linked back to specific analyses
Charts in Appendix 2 lack clear legends and axis labels
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets band 4 for number of documents but band 3 for relevance and depth. Documents are generally relevant and in-depth in places, but coverage is uneven and linkages to analysis could be strengthened.

strengths: Appropriate selection of 4Ps, ratio analysis, and Lewinâs force-field to address marketing, financial, and force dynamics
Application of tools shows sound connection to digital promotion and reputational costs
Force-field weights are explained in narrative
weaknesses: No explicit rationale for why chosen tools are the most suitable
Relevance of some financial ratios to marketing recovery is implicit rather than clearly articulated
Minor formula error and some unsupported claims (e.g., bundling strategy)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands are assessed at band 3. The choice and application of tools are adequate and generally relevant, but justification for selection is limited and some applications remain descriptive rather than critically evaluated.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effective use of multi-year audited data, tables, and visuals to track reputation-related trends
Accurate quoting of key financial figures and backlog statistics
Clear linkage of margin declines to reputational costs in places
weaknesses: Analysis often identifies trends without probing deeper causality or alternative interpretations
Integration across tools is patchy and narrative sections end abruptly
Data errors (currency symbols, rounding discrepancies) detract from analytical rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection meets band 4 standards, but analysis quality aligns with band 3. The overall performance averages to a band 3: there is sound evidence use but evaluation lacks sustained depth and integration.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question and references key findings from tools employed
Some consistency with evidence in summarizing 4Ps, ratios, and force-field outcomes
weaknesses: Overstates degree of reputational recovery without fully justifying âhigh extentâ claim
Inconsistency between force-field balance and conclusionâs positive tone
Language could be tempered and evidence linkage strengthened
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion meets band 3 for explicitly answering the research question but only band 2 for consistency with evidence. The lower mark reflects uneven justification and some over-generalisation of results.

strengths: Logical IB IA structure with introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, and appendices
Table of contents and clear section headings guide the reader
weaknesses: Minor formatting glitches (LaTeX remnants, duplicated headings)
Incomplete sentences in conclusion section that interrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure is appropriate and coherent (band 2). Small formatting and completeness issues do not impede overall organization but prevent a lower-band deduction.

strengths: All required presentation elements are present: title page, word count, labelled sections, citations, bibliography, and supporting documents
Consistent in-text citation and bibliography inclusion
weaknesses: Inconsistent reference formatting and italics usage
LaTeX code remnants and missing section headers in some appendices
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fully meets the highest band criteria (band 2) for required elements, with only polishing-level issues in formatting and consistency.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical IB IA structure with introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography and appendices, aided by a table of contents.  Minor formatting glitches (e.g., LaTeX remnants, duplicated headings) reduce polish but do not impede understanding, so overall structure is appropriate (band 2).

Most documents (annual reports, supply-chain article, marketing-strategy blog) relate directly to Airbusâs reputation and performance, providing useful quantitative and qualitative material.  Depth varies: the financial statements are rich, but some blog-style sources give surface-level commentary.  Coverage is therefore ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than wide-ranging and in-depth, matching band 3.

All required elementsâtitle page with RQ and word count, labelled sections, in-text citations, bibliography, and supporting documentsâare present.  Therefore the work meets the highest band for presentation.
Five clearly labelled supporting documents are supplied in the appendices, satisfying the 3-5 requirement at the top band for this strand.

Application is generally soundâe.g., 4Ps linked to digital promotion, ratio trends tied to reputation costs, and force-field weights explained.  However, relevance is not always explicit (the liquidity ratios are described but not clearly tied back to marketing recovery) and some analysis remains descriptive.  This aligns with âadequately appliedâ in band 3.

The student selects multiple years of audited financial data, revenue charts, and a visual summary of orders and deliveriesâclearly pertinent to reputation recovery.  Data are quoted accurately in both tables and narrative, showing âsufficientâ rather than exemplary breadth; hence band 4 for effective selection and use is appropriate.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâfor instance, linking net-profit margin decline to reputational costs and using force-field scores to judge balance.  Still, evaluation tends to stop at identifying trends without probing causality or offering alternative interpretations, and integration across tools is patchy.  This fits âsome analysis of the research questionâ (band 3) rather than the mostly effective, integrated standard of band 4.
The research question is explicitly answered in the conclusion section and the extent is qualified, satisfying the descriptor for an explicit answer (band 3).

The choice of 4Ps, ratio analysis and Lewinâs Force-Field provides an *adequate* toolkit to address marketing effectiveness, financial impact and the balance of forces.  While appropriate, the toolkit is limited (no stakeholder mapping, no brand-equity metrics) and lacks justification of why each was the most suitable, so selection is competent rather than fully âeffectiveâ.

The conclusion states that Airbus has recovered its reputation âto a high extentâ and references key findings from the 4Ps, ratios and force-field, showing partial consistency with presented evidence.  Yet several claims (e.g., âhigh extentâ despite equal driving/restraining forces) are not fully justified, so alignment with evidence is unevenâmatching band 2.

Discussion is consistently anchored in Airbus: detailed background on the A350, use of Airbus annual financial statements, and organisation-specific marketing actions (Paris Air Show, SAF partnerships, Emirates order, etc.).  Analytical comments connect these specifics to the research question rather than remaining generic.  Nevertheless, some points slip into description (e.g., listing product ranges without evaluating their effect on reputation), so the assessment is strongly but only *partially* integrated â aligned with band 4.

The student keeps the focus squarely on the key concept of **change**, referencing it in the research question, methodology and every analytical section (e.g., linking supply-chain setbacks to âneed for changeâ in the Force-Field and 4Ps discussion).  The narrative shows clear insight into how marketing activities had to evolve after the A350 delays and ties this back to reputational recovery.  However, much of the integration is done through repeated tagging of the word âchangeâ rather than fully weaving the concept into deeper, sustained analytical chains (for example, the ratio analysis is only lightly related back to reputational change).  This places the work above âgood understandingâ (band 3) but still short of the âfully integrated throughoutâ standard needed for band 5.

annotations.comment: The Supply Chain World visual is a strong addition that supports the supply chain analysis with real data.
This appendix paragraph lacks a heading. Add a clear section header to maintain consistency and reader orientation.
The research question is clearly stated and focused on Airbusâs marketing strategies and reputation recovery, aligning well with the IA requirements.
Typographical error in the research question: correct âlevergedâ to âleveragedâ for professionalism and clarity.
The key concept of âchangeâ is explicitly identified and central, providing a consistent thematic focus for the investigation.
LaTeX remnants in the table of contents (e.g., â\textbfâ) reduce polish. Remove or replace these markers to improve presentation.
The introduction provides background but does not explicitly link Airbusâs context to the key concept of change. Integrate the concept here to strengthen coherence.
The term âbacklog of over 8,500 aircraft ordersâ conflates orders with undelivered backlog. Clarify whether this figure refers to outstanding orders or actual production delays.
The methodology lists appropriate tools but lacks justification for why these specific tools best address the research question. Add rationale for your selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The mention of supporting articles is vague. Specify which documents contribute to each analytical tool and how their content underpins your analysis.
The description of Product is informative but does not explicitly tie product features back to reputation recovery. Explain how product innovations restored trust.
The Price section heading is missing, causing a structural gap. Add the â3.2.3 Priceâ header to maintain clear organization.
The discussion of bundling and package deals lacks a source. Provide a citation or example to support this claim about pricing strategy adaptation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Promotion examples are vivid but there is no evaluation of their effectiveness. Include metrics or stakeholder feedback to assess impact.
Financial figures are reported in sterling and yen for Airbus, which uses euros. Correct the currency symbols to euros (â¬) for accuracy.
The Acid Test Ratio formula should read $$\frac{\text{Current Assets} - \text{Inventories}}{\text{Current Liabilities}}$$. Update the expression to avoid confusion.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The narrative after the tables ends abruptly, leaving the analysis incomplete. Expand on how margin trends connect to reputation recovery.
The text states net profit margin declined to 7.06% in 2023, while the table shows 7.03%. Align figures or explain rounding discrepancy.
The force field diagram lists equal driving and restraining forces but does not justify the scoring method. Explain the weighting criteria.
Describing titanium as a semiconductor is incorrect; classify it accurately as a metal used in airframe components.
The description of stakeholder involvement is vague. Specify which stakeholders were engaged and how they contributed to A350 development.
The conclusion claims reputation recovered âto a high extentâ but evidence supports only partial recovery. Temper the language or add stronger justification.
The conclusion repeats âWith the 4Ps of Marketingâ¦â and ends mid-sentence. Revise for conciseness, eliminate duplication, and complete all thoughts.
The suggestion to conduct qualitative interviews in future research is valuable and demonstrates forward thinking for stakeholder insights.
Bibliography entries vary in format and italics usage. Standardize reference style and ensure consistency across all sources.
Appendix 1 is included but not explicitly linked back to which analysis it supports. In the methodology, note how each document informs your tools.
The comparison chart from supporting document 2 lacks a clear legend and axis labels. Add these for better interpretation.
Appendix 3 presents full financial statements but could be summarized to highlight key reputation indicators, improving readability.
The financial position section is detailed and demonstrates sufficient data selection to support analysis.
The section header is wordy and technical; consider simplifying it to improve readability (e.g., âStatement of Financial Position 2023â2022â).
Appendix 4 shows â4.213â where â4,213â is intended. Correct number formatting to match thousands separators.
The footnote explaining restatement of provisions is clear and enhances transparency in the financial analysis.
The appendix text on supply chain impact ends abruptly. Complete the final sentence to ensure the discussion is not cut off.
